statement: Every ignorance demonstrated by the LLM in pi must be explicitly accounted for, such that no LLM in pi has that ignorance — demonstrated LLM ignorance is a design input the substrate must encode against, not an incident to move past
recurrence: stated once mid-turn 2026-05-22; distinct from IN-29 (task an agent for a gap's root cause) and IN-94 (enforcement over prompting) in demanding a proactive, explicit accounting of each demonstrated ignorance class so it cannot recur in any future in-pi LLM

history.note: completeness sweep of the 52 never-read mid-turn user statements (attachments), 2026-05-10 to 2026-07-13

group.name: Governing quality metric — best-of-breed / superior UX

statement: Target state: pi-context is a first-rate command — a properly installed command, not node /path/to/bin invocation, with each command carrying a usage example so the schema need not be read for routine use, and with those examples kept from going stale
recurrence: twice mid-turn (2026-06-05 filing the first-rate-command target state; 2026-06-07 the per-command example + anti-staleness requirement); concretizes IN-111 (superior UX) and IN-77 (CLI single interface) into a specific declared CLI-ergonomics target state no existing atom names

history.note: completeness sweep of the 52 never-read mid-turn user statements (attachments), 2026-05-10 to 2026-07-13

statement: Dispatched agents must not sit idle — the orchestrator can certainly see whether an agent is idle, must check idle agents when they appear, and must answer why idle agents exist; idle agents are an orchestration failure, not background noise
recurrence: pressed five times within seven minutes mid-turn on 2026-07-11 (one idle, two idle, three, certainly you can see, why do we have idle agents); no existing atom addresses orchestrator monitoring/utilization of dispatched agents — IN-100 covers owning subagent output, not agent state

history.note: completeness sweep of the 52 never-read mid-turn user statements (attachments), 2026-05-10 to 2026-07-13

statement: The user's mid-work interjections cannot go unacknowledged — being told something twice without acknowledgment is itself a failure, regardless of what the assistant is working on at the time
recurrence: stated once mid-turn 2026-07-11, after the same report (idle agents) had been submitted twice without acknowledgment; distinct from IN-30/IN-31 (directive content fidelity) — this is an interaction-conduct norm that user reports must be acknowledged at all

history.note: completeness sweep of the 52 never-read mid-turn user statements (attachments), 2026-05-10 to 2026-07-13
